assertions.title: DNS, timeout, and unreachable-host failures are surfaced clearly

steps.action: Run the focused timeout failure flow through the real session UI on MainActivity against the SSH fixture endpoint
steps.expected: Timeout failure is surfaced distinctly, the app returns to a disconnected/non-live state, and the message is not confused with DNS or unreachable-host errors
steps.observed: SessionSshFixtureInstrumentedTest.real_session_ui_surfaces_timeout_distinctly_from_other_failure_messages passed and asserted 'Connection timed out while reaching 10.0.2.2:3122.', no pending trust, no connected state, disabled input, and a visible Reconnect action.

steps.action: Run the invalid-hostname failure flow on the instrumented session screen
steps.expected: DNS failure is surfaced clearly and remains disconnected
steps.observed: SessionRealConnectionFlowsInstrumentedTest.dns_failure_surfaces_clearly passed and surfaced 'DNS lookup failed' on the session status surface.

steps.action: Run the unreachable-host failure flow on the instrumented session screen
steps.expected: Unreachable-host failure is surfaced clearly and remains disconnected
steps.observed: SessionRealConnectionFlowsInstrumentedTest.network_failure_surfaces_clearly passed and surfaced 'Host is unreachable' on the session status surface.

evidence.network: SSH fixture was reachable from emulator at 10.0.2.2:3122 (fixture_exit:0); timeout, DNS, and unreachable-host failure messages were all validated by passing instrumentation flows.

assertions.title: Rotating or backgrounding during connection setup resolves to one truthful final state

steps.action: Start a real SSH fixture connection from the session tab in MainActivity and rotate during connection setup before trust is resolved
steps.expected: After lifecycle interruption there is still exactly one truthful in-progress state and the flow resolves to one final state
steps.observed: SessionSshFixtureInstrumentedTest.rotating_during_real_connect_setup_keeps_one_truthful_connected_state passed: after rotation there was one trust prompt, one disconnect button, a disabled Connecting… action for the host row, and accepting trust resolved to one connected state with remote proof on 10.0.2.2:3122.

steps.action: Start the same real fixture connection and background/resume the app during connection setup
steps.expected: Background/resume preserves one truthful connecting flow and resolves to one final state without duplicates or stale UI
steps.observed: SessionSshFixtureInstrumentedTest.backgrounding_and_resuming_during_real_connect_setup_keeps_one_truthful_connected_state passed with the same single trust-gated connecting state and one final connected state after resume.

steps.action: Inspect the resulting session surface after each lifecycle interruption
steps.expected: No duplicate sessions, stuck spinners, or misleading resumed state remain
steps.observed: Both flows ended with a single connected session, no duplicate trust prompts, and normal disconnect behavior afterward.

evidence.network: Real SSH fixture connection from emulator to 10.0.2.2:3122 remained truthful across rotation and background/resume during setup.

assertions.title: Editing an identity preserves or updates the real credential behavior correctly

steps.action: Connect with the saved password identity through the real app shell, then edit only identity metadata from the identities UI
steps.expected: Metadata-only edits preserve the stored secret and the next connection still works
steps.observed: SessionSshFixtureInstrumentedTest.real_fixture_session_ui_proves_trust_identity_edit_and_relaunch_through_visible_app_flows passed: renaming the identity in the identities UI updated the visible label, kept the stored secret, and the host reconnected successfully without another trust prompt.

steps.action: Replace the saved password secret with an incorrect value through the identities UI and reconnect
steps.expected: Secret replacement takes effect on the very next connection attempt and changed credentials alter authentication behavior
steps.observed: SessionSshFixtureInstrumentedTest.real_fixture_session_ui_truthfully_reflects_password_replacement_failures_and_recovery passed: replacing the password with 'wrong-password' caused the next connection attempt to fail with 'Authentication failed. Verify the linked identity and try again.' while the terminal remained non-live.

steps.action: Replace the secret back with the fixture password and reconnect again
steps.expected: Restoring the correct secret recovers real authentication behavior on the next attempt
steps.observed: The same rerun test then replaced the password back to the fixture value and the next reconnect succeeded to 10.0.2.2:3122 with remote proof, confirming secret updates are honored by real connection behavior.

evidence.network: Real SSH fixture authentication on 10.0.2.2:3122 succeeded before metadata edit, failed immediately after wrong-password replacement, and succeeded again after restoring the correct password.

frictions.description: The current instrumentation harness emits XML, HTML, and log artifacts but does not automatically produce screenshot files for these UI flows.

Current runnable evidence for VAL-CONNECT-004 is split across two instrumented surfaces: timeout was rerun through MainActivity, while DNS and unreachable-host messaging were rerun through the instrumented session screen test surface.
frictions.resolution: Used the best runnable user-surface evidence currently available and only marked pass after all three failure categories were covered by passing focused reruns.

summary: Retested 3 previously blocked assertions on emulator-5554 with focused instrumentation against the shared SSH fixture surface. PASS: VAL-CONNECT-004, VAL-CONNECT-011, VAL-IDENTITY-008. No assigned assertions remain blocked in this rerun.
